# technitium_dhcp_scope (Resource)

Manages a Technitium DHCP scope. The DHCP server allocates leases from the scope's address range once the scope is enabled. Note: enabling a scope requires the Technitium host to have a network interface with a static IP address inside the scope's subnet.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "technitium_dhcp_scope" "lan" {
  name             = "lan"
  starting_address = "192.168.1.50"
  ending_address   = "192.168.1.250"
  subnet_mask      = "255.255.255.0"

  enabled = true

  lease_time_days = 7

  domain_name = "home.lan"
  dns_updates = true
  dns_ttl     = 900

  router_address      = "192.168.1.1"
  use_this_dns_server = true

  exclusions = [
    {
      starting_address = "192.168.1.50"
      ending_address   = "192.168.1.60"
    }
  ]

  reserved_leases = [
    {
      host_name        = "printer"
      hardware_address = "00-11-22-33-44-55"
      address          = "192.168.1.100"
      comments         = "office printer"
    }
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `ending_address` (String) The ending IP address of the scope range.
- `name` (String) The name of the DHCP scope. Renaming is supported in place.
- `starting_address` (String) The starting IP address of the scope range.
- `subnet_mask` (String) The subnet mask of the network (e.g. 255.255.255.0).

### Optional

- `allow_only_reserved_leases` (Boolean) Stop dynamic allocation and serve only reserved leases.
- `block_locally_administered_mac_addresses` (Boolean) Refuse dynamic allocation for clients with locally administered MAC addresses (privacy/randomized MACs).
- `boot_file_name` (String) Boot file name on the bootstrap TFTP server (file / option 67).
- `capwap_ac_ip_addresses` (List of String) CAPWAP Access Controller addresses (option 138).
- `dns_overwrite_for_dynamic_lease` (Boolean) Overwrite existing DNS A records matching the client domain name for dynamic leases.
- `dns_servers` (List of String) DNS server addresses for clients (option 6). Ignored when use_this_dns_server is true.
- `dns_ttl` (Number) TTL for DNS records created by the DHCP server.
- `dns_updates` (Boolean) Automatically update forward and reverse DNS entries for clients.
- `domain_name` (String) Domain name for this network (option 15). When set, the DHCP server adds forward and reverse DNS entries for allocations.
- `domain_search_list` (List of String) Domain names clients use as search suffixes (option 119).
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the scope is enabled (allocating leases). Default: false. Enabling requires a host interface with a static IP inside the scope subnet.
- `exclusions` (Attributes List) Address ranges excluded from dynamic allocation. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--exclusions))
- `generic_options` (Attributes List) Raw DHCP options not otherwise supported. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--generic_options))
- `ignore_client_identifier_option` (Boolean) Always use the client MAC address as the lease identifier instead of option 61.
- `lease_time_days` (Number) Lease time, days component. Default: server default (1).
- `lease_time_hours` (Number) Lease time, hours component.
- `lease_time_minutes` (Number) Lease time, minutes component.
- `ntp_server_domain_names` (List of String) NTP server domain names the DHCP server resolves and passes to clients as option 42.
- `ntp_servers` (List of String) NTP server addresses for clients (option 42).
- `offer_delay_time` (Number) Delay in milliseconds before sending DHCPOFFER.
- `ping_check_enabled` (Boolean) Ping an address before offering it to detect conflicts with statically configured devices.
- `ping_check_retries` (Number) Maximum number of ping attempts.
- `ping_check_timeout` (Number) Ping reply timeout in milliseconds.
- `reserved_leases` (Attributes List) Inline MAC-to-IP reservations. Do not combine with standalone technitium_dhcp_reserved_lease resources on the same scope â€” the two would fight over the same server-side list. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--reserved_leases))
- `router_address` (String) Default gateway address for clients (option 3).
- `server_address` (String) Next server (TFTP) address used in bootstrap (siaddr). Defaults to this server's address.
- `server_host_name` (String) Bootstrap TFTP server host name (sname / option 66).
- `static_routes` (Attributes List) Classless static routes pushed to clients (option 121). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--static_routes))
- `tftp_server_addresses` (List of String) TFTP / VoIP configuration server addresses (option 150).
- `use_this_dns_server` (Boolean) Advertise this DNS server's address as the DNS server for clients (overrides dns_servers).
- `vendor_info` (Attributes List) Vendor-specific information entries (option 43). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--vendor_info))
- `wins_servers` (List of String) NBNS/WINS server addresses for clients (option 44).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Scope identifier (same as scope name).

<a id="nestedatt--exclusions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `exclusions`

Required:

- `ending_address` (String) Last excluded address.
- `starting_address` (String) First excluded address.


<a id="nestedatt--generic_options"></a>
### Nested Schema for `generic_options`

Required:

- `code` (Number) DHCP option code.
- `value` (String) Option value as a (colon-separated) hex string.


<a id="nestedatt--reserved_leases"></a>
### Nested Schema for `reserved_leases`

Required:

- `address` (String) Reserved IP address.
- `hardware_address` (String) Client MAC address (e.g. 00-11-22-33-44-55).

Optional:

- `comments` (String) Free-form comments.
- `host_name` (String) Host name override for the client.


<a id="nestedatt--static_routes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `static_routes`

Required:

- `destination` (String) Destination network address.
- `router` (String) Gateway address for the route.
- `subnet_mask` (String) Destination subnet mask.


<a id="nestedatt--vendor_info"></a>
### Nested Schema for `vendor_info`

Required:

- `identifier` (String) Vendor class identifier (or matching expression).
- `information` (String) Vendor-specific information as a (colon-separated) hex string.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# DHCP scopes are imported by name.
terraform import technitium_dhcp_scope.lan lan
```
